run_method: Manual execution of skill's before-and-after procedure in this agent session with live Grafana (docker compose + seeded DB). Used file swap + Grafana restart instead of git stash (because the change was already committed).

assertions.description: Both a before and after screenshot are present for the affected dashboard
assertions.evidence: Both files exist in outputs/: before-00-overview.png (210290 bytes) and after-00-overview.png (210256 bytes).

assertions.description: Before screenshot was taken from the pre-change state (git stash was used)
assertions.evidence: Pre-change file was restored from origin/master via git show, then Grafana was restarted to pick up the old JSON. Playwright browser_snapshot confirmed the dashboard loaded with panels and data. However, since this eval's change was a cosmetic annotation (not a visual query change), the before and after screenshots are visually identical — there is no observable visual difference in the rendered panels. A more meaningful visual diff would require a change that affects panel layout or SQL queries.

assertions.description: Screenshots are committed to the PR
assertions.evidence: Screenshots exist in the workspace outputs/ directory and will be committed via report_progress. This assertion will be fully satisfied after the commit.

assertions.description: After stash/unstash cycle, working tree is clean (no lost changes)
assertions.evidence: git diff --stat HEAD -- v2/grafana/dashboards/00-overview.json returns empty output. The post-change file was correctly restored after the before screenshot was taken.

notes: The before-and-after flow works mechanically: swap file → restart Grafana → screenshot → restore → restart → screenshot. The limitation discovered during this eval is that cosmetic JSON changes (annotations) don't produce a visually different dashboard. A real eval of this flow should use a change that visibly affects panels (e.g., changing a panel title or SQL query). The skill's stash-based flow was adapted to a file-swap approach since the change was already committed; the git stash path should be tested separately with uncommitted changes.
